# Phase 10 Runtime Authorization Boundary Plan

## Objective

Define the constitutional boundary between certification readiness and any future runtime authorization review without enabling runtime.

## Scope

- Runtime authorization boundary doctrine.
- Human approval sovereignty.
- Final activation gate philosophy.
- Certification-to-authorization separation.
- Activation non-autonomy.
- Runtime scope authorization standards.
- Reversible activation requirements.
- Shutdown and rollback preconditions.
- Authorization evidence packets.
- Shared-core runtime pressure protection.
- Industry-module containment.
- Treasury/runtime authorization separation.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- API creation.
- Provider onboarding.
- Adapter implementation.
- Credential creation.
- OAuth setup.
- Prisma or Supabase changes.
- Twilio or email execution.
- Background jobs.
- Activation systems.
- Authorization engines.
- Autonomous approval systems.
- Deployment authority.
- Industry-module execution.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Certification is not authorization.
- Authorization is not activation.
- Activation is not autonomous execution.
- Human approval remains sovereign.
- Shared core cannot be mutated by runtime pressure.
- Industry modules cannot bypass scoped adapters.
- Treasury and runtime authority remain separated.
- Shutdown, rollback, auditability, reversibility, and fail-closed behavior are required.
